original: Astronaut Zhai Zhigang becomes the first Chinese person to perform a spacewalk while flying on Shenzhou 7
The Mars Curiosity rover discovers what evidence of a fast-moving streambed-in Mars
Physics journal Annalen der Physik publishesAlbert Einstein's paper "Does the Inertia of a Body Depend Upon Its Energy Content?", introducing the equation E=mc².
Circus performer James Cooke walks a 150 yard tightrope from Cliff House to Seal Rocks in San Francisco
1st Santa Claus Training School opens (Albion NY)
1st NHL exhibition game at Nassau Coliseum; NY Rangers beat NY Islanders, 6-4
Canadian Finn class sailor Lawrence Lemieux abandons race 5 to assist injured competitor after being in 2nd place; selfless act is awarded with the Pierre de Coubertin medal
Smart 1 satellite is launched by the European Space Agency from the Guiana Space Centre in Kourou, French Guiana, for a moon orbit.
Nolan Ryanstrikes out 16 in 11 innings, for record 383 of season
French scholar Jean-François Champollion announces he has deciphered Egyptian hieroglyphs using the Rosetta Stone
Stephanie D. Wilson, American NASA astronaut (STS-121, STS-120, STS-131), born in Boston, Massachusetts
George Stephenson's "Locomotion No. 1" becomes the first steam locomotive to carry passengers on a public rail line, the Stockton and Darlington Railway in England
American diverGreg Louganiswins the 10m platform gold medal at the Seoul Olympics; wraps up diving double after also taking out the 3m springboard gold
Mamie "Peanut" Johnson, American baseball pitcher (1st female pitcher to play in the Negro Leagues), born in Ridgeway, South Carolina (d. 2017)
1st Native American Day celebrated, honoring American Indians
American swimmer Johnny Weismuller sets his first 2 world records in the 100m and 150yd freestyle events the A.A.U. Nationals meet in Brighton Beach, NY
W. C. Handypublishes "Memphis Blues," considered the first blues song
Rachel Carsonpublishes "Silent Spring" about the deleterious impacts of pesticide use in the US on the environment
United States baseball team, managed byTommy Lasorda, wins Olympic Gold Medal in Sydney, Australia
Soyuz 12 carries 2 cosmonauts into Earth orbit (2 days)
Hurricane Gloria's 130 MPH wind hits Atlantic coast
The 2004 Japanese discovery of the 133th element has been confirmed
Henry Ford's first Ford Model T automobile leaves the Piquette Plant in Detroit, Michigan
Bernard Waber, American children's book author ("The House on East 88th Street"; "Lyle, Lyle, Crocodile"), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2013)
Robert Edwards, English physiologist, 1st IVF program, Nobel Prize 2010, born in Batley, Yorkshire (d. 2013)[1]
Steve Kerr, American basketball guard, head coach (5 NBA titles as player Chicago Bulls, SA Spurs - 3 as coach GS Warriors), born in Beirut, Lebanon
Typhoon destroys one third of Nagoya, Japan
William the Conqueror's troops set sail from Normandy to conquer England
Dutch Moluccan fleet of the Fifth Expedition, under command of Wolfert Harmensz, land on Mauritius suffering from scurvy, where they hunt and document the dodo
Elephantine Colossus, a vacant seven-story building in the shape of an elephant built in 1885, burns to the ground in Coney Island, New York
American MLB San Francisco Giants rent Pacific Coast League San Francisco Seals Stadium until Candlestick Park is built in 1960
John Adamsis appointed to negotiate Revolutionary War peace terms with Great Britain
Boston Red Sox sluggerBabe Ruthtakes his MLB home run record to 29 with a 3rd inning blast in a 7-5 defeat at the Washington Senators' Griffith Stadium
MLB Detroit Tigers rookie pitcher Floyd Giebells throws 2-0 shut-out over Cleveland IndiansBob Fellerto clinch the AL pennant at League Park in Cleveland, Ohio
WWII: US General and head of the Allied occupation of Japan,Douglas MacArthur, meets Emperor Hirohito in Tokyo for the first time
Harry Blackstone Sr., American magician, born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 1965)
Martin Ryle, English radio astronomer (Astronomer Royal 1972-82, Nobel Prize for Physics 1974), born in Brighton, England (d. 1984)[1]
Will Sampson, American-Muscogee rodeo performer, actor (One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest: The Yellow Rose; The White Buffalo), and painter, born in Okmulgee, Oklahoma (d. 1987)
Robbie Shakespeare, Jamaican reggae bassist (Sly & Robbie - "DJ Riot"), born in Kingston, Jamaica (d. 2021)
1st African American Supreme Court page is CV Bush
1st test flight of a twin-engined airplane (France)
Balinese Tiger declared extinct
WMAZ TV channel 13 in Macon, GA (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
School integration begins in Washington, D.C. & Baltimore Md public schools
Guillaume Rondelet, French naturalist famous for his study of marine animals, born in Montpellier, France (d. 1566)
(Earl) "Bud" Powell, American jazz pianist and composer, born in Harlem, New York City (d. 1966)
Margaret Rule, British archaeologist (Mary Rose Tudor warship), born in Buckinghamshire, England (d. 2015)
T.C. Cannon, Native American artist (Kiowa/Caddo), born in Lawton, Oklahoma (d. 1978)
Diane Abbott, British politician (L) 1st black woman elected to UK parliament, born in London
Wreck of the Old 97, a train crash made famous by the song of the same name.
British ocean liner "Queen Elizabeth" launches at Clydebank, Scotland
Black leaders protest discrimination in US armed forces
Europe's 1st "moving pavement" (travelator), opens at Bank station on the London Underground
57 people are killed after Mount Ontake erupts in Japan
US PresidentWilliam Howard Taftsets aside some 3 million acres of oil-rich public land (including Teapot Dome, Wyoming) for conservation purposes
US President Roosevelt launches the 1st Liberty ship, freighter SSPatrick Henry
Heavyweight championEzzard CharlesdefeatsJoe Louisin 15 in Yankee Stadium, Bronx, New York
Filming begins on "Never Say Never Again" withSean Conneryplaying James Bond for the final time
Lordereleases her debut studio album "Pure Heroine"
George Aiken's play "Uncle Tom's Cabin" an adaptation ofHarriet Beecher Stowe's famous anti-slavery novel premieres in Troy, New York
